AI adoption in Aotearoa New Zealand is furthest along where it augments routine work inside existing workflows—most visibly in healthcare’s nationwide emergency-department scribes and agriculture’s embedded farm tools—while much of the public sector and other areas remain earlier-stage, focused on lower-risk productivity and decision support rather than autonomous frontline judgement. Across sectors, the common constraints are trust, skills, privacy, data quality, and integration, with concrete use centred on clinical documentation, fraud and anomaly detection, workflow automation, and herd-health or pasture management.

This living whitepaper currently tracks AI in academic research, agriculture, creative industries, education, finance, healthcare, and the public sector in Aotearoa New Zealand.

Updated 11 March 2026
